inline_comments.comment: The documented default is `true`, but the actual default set in `common-preset.ts` is `false`, and the JSDoc in `core-common.ts` also declares `@default false`. This contradiction will mislead users who rely on the documentation. Change `Default: \`true\`` to `Default: \`false\``.

The inline `TODO: Turn to true before 10.4 release` comment is easy to overlook and has no tracking reference. Consider replacing it with a link to a GitHub issue (e.g., `TODO(#<issue-number>): Enable by default before 10.4 release`) so it is discoverable and actionable, following the pattern used by other temporary flags like `legacyDecoratorFileOrder`.

general_comments.comment: The `changeDetection` flag is declared and given a default value, but no code in this diff (or found elsewhere in the codebase) actually reads `global.FEATURES?.changeDetection`. Every other feature flag in `common-preset.ts` (e.g., `componentsManifest`, `sidebarOnboardingChecklist`) has corresponding consuming code that gates real behavior behind the flag. Without a consumer, this flag is inert and enables nothing. If the consuming code is in a separate PR, that should be noted; otherwise the flag addition is incomplete.
